message:
🌱 Refactor all cards to use unified useCardLoadingState hook (#469)

* ⚡ Optimize scroll position restoration to reduce forced reflows

Performance improvements to useLastRoute hook:

- Reduced max restore attempts from 40 to 10 (6s → 1s max)
- Reduced min attempts from 8 to 3 for faster stabilization
- Increased position tolerance from 2px to 5px
- Reduced retry delay from 150ms to 100ms
- Increased scroll debounce from 500ms to 1000ms

These changes reduce forced reflows caused by repeated
getBoundingClientRect() calls during dashboard navigation,
which was contributing to slow dashboard switches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Add useCardLoadingState hook and refactor cards

- Add useCardLoadingState hook to CardDataContext.tsx that abstracts
  the common loading/caching boilerplate pattern
- Refactor ~50 cards to use the new hook, eliminating repetitive code
- Hook provides consistent stale-while-revalidate behavior
- Returns showSkeleton boolean for skeleton condition
- Supports isFailed and consecutiveFailures for cached hooks
- Remaining 28 cards still use useReportCardDataState

The new pattern reduces this boilerplate:
  const hasData = !isLoading || data.length > 0
  useReportCardDataState({
    isFailed: false,
    consecutiveFailures: 0,
    isLoading: isLoading && !hasData,
    isRefreshing: isLoading && hasData,
    hasData,
  })

To this:
  const { showSkeleton } = useCardLoadingState({
    isLoading,
    hasAnyData: data.length > 0,
  })

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Refactor ArgoCDHealth and GPUUtilization to useCardLoadingState

Continue unified card loading hook refactoring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🌱 Refactor remaining cards to use useCardLoadingState hook

Completes the unified card loading solution by refactoring all 26
remaining cards from useReportCardDataState to useCardLoadingState.
This eliminates ~200 lines of boilerplate code and ensures consistent
stale-while-revalidate behavior across all cards.

Cards refactored:
- ArgoCDApplications, ArgoCDSyncStatus, ClusterDropZone
- ComplianceCards, DataComplianceCards, GatewayStatus
- HelmReleaseStatus, Kubectl, KustomizationStatus
- NamespaceQuotas, NamespaceRBAC, OperatorSubscriptions
- ResourceMarshall, ServiceExports, ServiceImports
- UserManagement, console-missions/* (4 cards)
- workload-detection/* (4 cards), workload-monitor/* (2 cards)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -44,3 +44,69 @@ export function useReportCardDataState(state: CardDataState) {
     ctx.report({ isFailed, consecutiveFailures, isLoading, isRefreshing, hasData })
   }, [ctx, isFailed, consecutiveFailures, isLoading, isRefreshing, hasData])
 }
+
+/**
+ * Options for useCardLoadingState hook
+ */
+export interface CardLoadingStateOptions {
+  /** Whether data is currently being fetched from the source */
+  isLoading: boolean
+  /** Whether the card has any data to display (e.g., data.length > 0) */
+  hasAnyData: boolean
+  /** Whether 3+ consecutive fetch failures have occurred (default: false) */
+  isFailed?: boolean
+  /** Number of consecutive fetch failures (default: 0) */
+  consecutiveFailures?: number
+}
+
+/**
+ * Simplified hook for cards to report loading state with correct stale-while-revalidate behavior.
+ *
+ * This hook handles the common pattern where:
+ * - `hasData` should be true once loading completes (even with empty data)
+ * - `hasData` should be true if we have cached data (even while refreshing)
+ * - Skeleton should only show when loading AND no cached data exists
+ *
+ * @example
+ * ```tsx
+ * const { clusters, isLoading } = useClusters()
+ * const { showSkeleton } = useCardLoadingState({
+ *   isLoading,
+ *   hasAnyData: clusters.length > 0,
+ * })
+ *
+ * if (showSkeleton) {
+ *   return <CardSkeleton type="list" rows={3} />
+ * }
+ * ```
+ */
+export function useCardLoadingState(options: CardLoadingStateOptions) {
+  const {
+    isLoading,
+    hasAnyData,
+    isFailed = false,
+    consecutiveFailures = 0,
+  } = options
+
+  // hasData is true once loading completes (even with empty data) OR if we have cached data
+  // This prevents flickering when data array is momentarily empty during refresh
+  const hasData = !isLoading || hasAnyData
+
+  // Report state to CardWrapper for refresh animation and status badges
+  useReportCardDataState({
+    isFailed,
+    consecutiveFailures,
+    isLoading: isLoading && !hasData,
+    isRefreshing: isLoading && hasData,
+    hasData,
+  })
+
+  return {
+    /** Whether the card has data to display (true once loading completes or has cached data) */
+    hasData,
+    /** Whether to show skeleton loading state (only when loading with no cached data) */
+    showSkeleton: isLoading && !hasAnyData,
+    /** Whether data is being refreshed (has cache, fetching update) */
+    isRefreshing: isLoading && hasData,
+  }
+}
